title: Kitster Chanchani Banai

{{Quote|This is so wizard, Ani!|Kitster|<ref name="TPM" />}}
'''Kitster Chanchani Banai''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Slavery/Legends|slave]] boy on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], and the best friend of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]]. After not seeing his [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] father, [[Rakir Banai]], in many years, Kitster gave up hopes of being rescued from Tatooine and instead hoped to one day be a [[Majordomo/Legends|majordomo]] for a wealthy [[Mos Espa/Legends|Mos Espan]] estate.

==Biography==
===Childhood===
{{Quote|Years ago I had a friend -- a slave, like I was -- who later left Tatooine and became a great Jedi Knight.|Kitster Banai, to a spacer|<ref name="SWG:KB">{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=static|Kitster Banai|Tatooine}}</ref>}}
Kitster Chanchani Banai was born the son of smuggler [[Rakir Banai]],<ref name="DB" /> circa [[41 BBY/Legends|41 BBY]].<ref name="age" /> Rakir was wanted by the [[Hutt Cartel/Legends|Hutts]], who eventually tracked him down and captured his ship with his family on board. As a result, Kitster and his mother were sold into slavery,<ref name="TCSWE-Rakir">{{CSWECite|I|57|Banai, Rakir}}</ref> which brought them to the [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] city of [[Mos Espa/Legends|Mos Espa]].<ref name="TCSWE-Kitster">{{CSWECite|I|57|Banai, Kitster Chanchani}}</ref>
[[File:WaldKittsterCringe-TPM.png|thumb|left|250px|Kitster and [[W. Wald|Wald]] watching the [[Boonta Eve Classic/Legends|Boonta Eve Classic]] in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]].]]
A short time before the [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]] [[Boonta Eve/Legends|Boonta's Eve]], the young Kitster, with [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] and their friends, narrowly avoided disaster upon hearing of children who had been captured and imprisoned by [[Sebulba/Legends|Sebulba]]. The [[Dug/Legends|Dug]] intended to sell the captives, including Princess [[Arawynne]], to [[Gardulla Besadii the Elder/Legends|Gardulla the Elder]]. The children, disguised as [[Jawa/Legends|Jawas]], sneaked into Gardulla's estate and freed the [[Ghostling]] children.<ref name="TGC">''[[Episode I Adventures 5: The Ghostling Children]]''</ref>

Kitster was the best friend of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] on Tatooine,<ref name="TCSWE-Kitster" /> but he was also a slave—property of Gardulla, who was ever threatening to sell him to the [[Glitterstim/Legends|glitterstim]] [[Miner/Legends|miners]] if he didn't "shape up."<ref name="TGC" /> Both boys (then unaware of the true nature of that order) once revered the Sith based on a holo-recording they had seen replayed by a Sith droid Watto had somehow acquired. Their games of "[[Sith Warrior]]" came to an end when an old spacer explained the true nature and history of the Sith.<ref name="Star Wars Roleplaying Game Core Rulebook">''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Core Rulebook]]''</ref> During the [[Podracing/Legends|podrace]] on Boonta Eve, Kitster was part of Anakin's pit crew.<ref name="TCSWE-Kitster" />

Before Anakin left to become a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], he left Kitster some of his prize credits. With it, Kitster bought ''[[Par Ontham's Guide to Etiquette]]'', a book that Kitster memorized to secure a job at the [[Three Moons (hotel)|Three Moons]], run by [[Rarta Dal]]. With that job, Kitster earned enough credits to buy his own freedom by the time he had grown up. It wasn't too long before Shmi Skywalker herself was freed by and married to [[Cliegg Lars/Legends|Cliegg Lars]]; Kitster attended the wedding.<ref name="Tatooine Ghost" />

[[22 BBY/Legends|Ten years]] after Anakin had gone, Kitster got word that his old friend had returned, asking Watto about his mother. He travelled to the [[Lars homestead/Legends|Lars homestead]], on the other side of [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]], in the hopes of seeing Anakin again, only to miss him and learn of Shmi being captured and tortured to death by [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raiders]]. [[Beru Whitesun Lars/Legends|Beru Whitesun]], the girlfriend of [[Owen Lars/Legends|Owen Lars]], relayed to him Anakin's lament about not being "strong enough" twice at Shmi's grave, even his promise to not "fail again". It would take almost three decades before Kitster figured out what that second "wasn't strong enough" meant.<ref name="Tatooine Ghost" />

===Bandrik wrath===
{{Quote|I've been trying to help Tatooine's slaves get better treatment. This offends a slaver named Drik Bandrik.|Kitster Banai, to a spacer|<ref name="SWG:KB" />}}
Kitster was seen at Mos Espa<ref name="SWG:KB" /> in [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]].<ref name="SWG_timeline">{{SWG_timeline}}</ref> He did not know at the time that his best friend had fallen to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] and become the dreaded Darth Vader. In fact, he believed Anakin had died during the Clone Wars.<ref name="SWG:KB" />
[[File:KitsterBanai.png|thumb|The adult Kitster Banai during times of trouble]]

When Kitster attempted to improve the lives of a group of slaves, he ended up offending their owner, the slaver [[Drik Bandrik]]. Angered, Bandrik framed Kitster by manipulating a tribe of violent [[Jawa/Legends|Jawas]] into believing that Kitster had stolen a droid from them. Luckily for Kitster, a benevolent [[Spacer/Legends|traveler]] agreed to help Kitster and kill the group of aggressive Jawas, all of whom were working for [[Wittin/Legends|Wittin]]. As a reward, Kitster gave the traveler Anakin's podracing helmet. However, Kitster's problems were not yet over.<ref name="SWG:KB" />

Bandrik had once again framed Kitster, this time planting evidence with [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt]] that implicated Kitster with attempting to lead a slave revolt at [[Jabba's Palace/Legends|his palace]] a few years ago. Though a revolt did take place, and fail, Jabba was still enraged enough to send a [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Assassin/Legends|henchman]] named [[Deeng]] to kill Kitster. The same traveler who had helped him before agreed to hunt down and kill Deeng.<ref name="SWG:KB" />

Though the traveler succeeded in killing Deeng, Bandrik's persistence in framing Kitster did not end there. This time Kitster was being implicated in the murder of a [[Moisture farmer/Legends|moisture farmer]]. Kitster insisted that he was nowhere near the murder and had the evidence to prove it. Kitster's alibi was that he was rotting in an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Prison/Legends|jail]] cell during the time of the murder. He was arrested for "political agitation," as a result of his attempts to promote slave rights. Ultimately, the Galactic Empire released Kitster, because they believed him to be harmless. However, [[Sont Toipo]], a local vigilante leader on Bandrik's payroll, was already rallying a lynch mob to kill Kitster. Kitster surmised that the mob consisted of six or eight local moisture farmers. He pleaded with the traveler to kill Sont Toipo, but to make an effort to spare the others, as they were merely farmers. Kitster hoped they would run off after Toipo was killed. The traveler agreed, and set out to successfully kill Toipo, while making an effort to spare the lives of the farmers. For the traveler's efforts, Kitster gave away another souvenir, a [[Droid brain/Legends|droid brain]] built by his old friend Anakin.<ref name="SWG:KB" />

[[File:Kitster-adult.jpg|thumb|left|Grown-up Kitster]]
Eventually, Kitster learned that the "murdered" moisture farmer was in fact not murdered at all, but rather paid by Bandrik to leave town long enough for a lynch mob to be formed. In order to clear his name, Kitster asked the traveler to find the moisture farmer, a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] named [[Krin Vel]], and bring her back to Mos Espa. Despite being ambushed by thugs working for Bandrik, the traveler managed to find Krin, and escort her back. Krin claimed that she did not know she was being used in a plot against Kitster. She wished to be brought back, so that both Kitster and herself could see to it that Bandrik went to prison.<ref name="SWG:KB" />

With the help of Krin, Kitster managed to assemble enough evidence that would clear him of Bandrik's fake charges. He considered sending the evidence to the [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|police]], but soon changed his mind when he surmised that the police and the Empire would have no reason to arrest him. He instead told the traveler to bring the evidence to a [[Slicer/Legends|slicer]] friend, a [[Kitonak/Legends|Kitonak]] who went by the name [[Thermal Det]]. The traveler agreed to Kitster's final task and succeeded in finding Thermal and giving him the evidence. Thermal saw to it that the information went straight to Jabba and made it onto the [[HoloNet/Legends|HoloNet]]. Kitster thanked the traveler once again for helping him. Kitster concluded that Jabba would be angered with Bandrik for being manipulated. He believed that Bandrik would have to leave the planet if he ever hoped to survive.<ref name="SWG:KB" />

Ironically, Kitster's father, Rakir Banai, was working for Bandrik during these events. Bandrik personally tasked Rakir to deal with the troublemaker, without informing him of his identity. Thanks to the help of a spacer, Rakir finally discovered that Bandrik was a slaver and that his long missing son was the troublemaker. Rakir later send out messengers to the various cities of Tatooine to find Kitster.<ref name="SWG:RB">{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=static|Rakir Banai|Tatooine}}</ref>

===Anakin's legacy===
{{Quote|The boy I knew would have been sorry for what he had done, and even ten years would not have changed that. He was still his mother's son.|Kitster, to Leia Organa Solo|<ref name="Tatooine Ghost" />}}
Eventually, Kitster married a local [[Kuati/Legends|Kuati]] woman named [[Ulda]], who maintained the old [[Mos Espa Grand Arena/Legends|Podracing Arena]], eventually turning it into a [[Swoop/Legends|swoop]] bike arena. However, Kitster quickly fell in love with the arena's bartender, [[Tamora Spice]]. Kitster left Ulda and married [[Tamora Spice|Tamora]], and together they had two kids, [[Ji Banai|Ji]] and [[Elly Banai|Elly]].<ref name="Tatooine Ghost" />

With Tamora by his side, Kitster soon accomplished his ultimate goal in life by becoming the [[Majordomo/Legends|majordomo]] at the [[Rendala Estate]] in Mos Espa. However, by [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]] the estate had been foreclosed, leaving Kitster unemployed. Times became so hard that Kitster took to auctioning off personal objects, such as a [[Holocube/Legends|holocube]] of his old friend Anakin Skywalker after winning the [[Boonta Eve Classic/Legends|Boonta Eve Classic]]. The holocube was sold to a [[Gotal/Legends|Gotal]] collector for thirteen hundred [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] at the same auction as the famous painting ''[[Killik Twilight]]'', which held [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] codes in it.<ref name="Tatooine Ghost" />

[[File:Tatooine_ghost_illustration_scene.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Older Kitster meeting with the disguised Solos]]
In [[8 ABY/Legends|8.5 ABY]], he [[Mission to Tatooine (Killik Twilight)|met]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia]] and [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] at the same auction. When ''Killik Twilight'' was threatened with destruction, Kitster acted fast, stealing the painting and using his old friend [[W. Wald|Wald's]] [[Swoop/Legends|swoop]] bike to escape into the deserts from pursuing [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]]. Kitster hid in the [[Arch Canyon/Legends|Arch Canyon]]. When a huge sandstorm struck, Kitster took shelter in a [[Jawa/Legends|Jawa]] [[Sandcrawler/Legends|Sandcrawler]] that eventually was attacked by Tusken Raiders. The Sand People kidnapped Kitster and the painting and brought them back to the [[Valley of the Spirits]], where they planned on sacrificing him to the "angry ghost" that had [[Mission to rescue Shmi Skywalker Lars/Legends|slain]] an entire tribe with a "[[Lightsaber/Legends|glowing, humming sword]]." The Tuskens began to [[Torture/Legends|torture]] Kitster as part of the sacrifice by breaking his fingers for every time the moss painting needed to be watered. Fortunately, within a few days, Han and Leia found and rescued him and the painting. Before parting, Kitster explained his beliefs that Anakin must have always regretted his vengeful attack on the Tuskens, even after becoming the feared [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], which eventually brought Leia to conclude that she should forgive her father.<ref name="Tatooine Ghost" />

==Behind the scenes==
Kitster Banai was portrayed by [[Dhruv Chanchani]] in [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''The Phantom Menace'']].<ref name="TPM" /> "Chanchani" was later given as his middle name in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.<ref name="TCSWE-Kitster" />
